summary.objective: Pressure SC-CONCEPT-0006 with a discriminating benchmark that tests whether invariant-guided symbolic-map extraction beats a looser symbolic-description baseline.
summary.whyNow: The active-frontier compression pass selected symbolic maps / narrative invariants as the next proof-bearing move after establishing the proof-path ladder.
summary.operatorReading: The frontier is well chosen and better framed than before, but it is still under-evidenced until the benchmark is actually run and scored.

mathematical.name: Structured representation and scoring over repeated artifacts
mathematical.notes: The benchmark assumes a stable structured extraction can be compared and scored, but the extraction structure is only described narratively rather than frozen as a formal schema.

mathematical.name: Comparative benchmark rubric over small bounded artifact set
mathematical.notes: The 0-2 scoring rubric is stated, but no formal validation or inter-rater procedure exists yet.

physical.name: None directly load-bearing for this frontier
physical.notes: This frontier is currently interpretive/representation-focused rather than physics-bearing. Its legitimacy depends more on benchmark discipline than on physical postulates.

modeling.name: Narrative invariants are a reusable internal substrate beneath symbolic maps
modeling.notes: This is the core Sandy Chaos modeling claim being pressured.

modeling.name: Proof-path ladder should govern current research pressure
modeling.notes: This governance layer is the reason this frontier exists now instead of lateral drift continuing.

modeling.name: A three-artifact manual-first benchmark is enough for first discrimination pressure
modeling.notes: Reasonable for v0, but still a modeling choice rather than a validated benchmark regime.

missingAssumptions: No frozen extraction template or schema artifact exists yet for Arm A.
No explicit inter-operator agreement assumption is tested in v0.
missingNullModels: A stronger alternative baseline beyond good-faith loose symbolic description is not yet specified.
missingBenchmarks: The benchmark has been specified but not yet executed on the three-artifact set.
No follow-on replication or broader corpus benchmark is defined yet.
missingArtifacts: Frozen extraction template for invariant-guided Arm A.
Result note comparing both arms across all three artifacts.
Pressure/update artifact reflecting benchmark outcome back into SC-CONCEPT-0006.
ambiguities: Artifact 3 selection rule exists, but the third artifact is not finalized in the benchmark spec.
contradictionRisks: The concept is described as validated-partial while its main discriminating benchmark is still pending, so wording may overstate current evidence if not handled carefully.
blockingGaps: Without the actual benchmark run, SC-CONCEPT-0006 remains mostly a Level-3 artifact family rather than a Level-4 discriminating result.

promotion.why: The frontier is correctly chosen and specified, but benchmark execution artifacts do not yet exist.

nextMove.action: Freeze the Arm A extraction template, choose artifact 3, then run both benchmark arms across all three artifacts.
nextMove.why: That is the smallest move that turns the current frontier from a good specification into a real discriminating test.
nextMove.expectedGain: Moves SC-CONCEPT-0006 from artifact-heavy plausibility toward actual Level-4 benchmark pressure.

handoff.why: Assistant-home continuity state already has the active Sandy Chaos proof-frontier baton, and its next action matches this frontier.
handoff.request: Resume the Sandy Chaos proof-frontier lane and continue by defining the symbolic-maps Level-4 discriminating benchmark for SC-CONCEPT-0006.
